interrogator_dossier:
# Forensic Dossier — freenet AG (FNTN.DE)

*Interrogator: Claude Opus 4.8 · Sector: Communication Services · Signal: deep_value (iv15) · Transcripts span 2024-05 (Q1'24) → 2025-11 (Q3'25), four calls, two CEO handovers*

---

## 1. NARRATIVE ARC

freenet's core story is remarkably stable across the window — and that stability is itself the most important finding. It is a German MVNO (no owned network; resells DT/Vodafone/Telefonica capacity) plus a growth IPTV asset (waipu.tv) plus a structurally-declining terrestrial-TV/broadcast tail (freenet TV / Media Broadcast). The whole investment debate hinges on the cross-over: **can waipu.tv's EBITDA contribution outgrow the freenet-TV runoff fast enough to keep group EBITDA on its "≥€600m by 2028" glide path while mobile stays roughly flat.**

- **Q1'24 (Vilanek):** "everything on plan and a bit boring… I'm happy that it's true." The arc opens defensively honest: mobile growth "slows down… more and more stability in the market"; waipu is the growth engine (record net adds ~139k); Gravis (Apple retail) is being **shut down** — the first quiet admission that a legacy distribution leg is dead. Capital-allocation frame: 80% of FCF to dividend, remainder to buyback.
- **FY'24 (Mar'25):** Victory-lap tone — "record year," EBITDA €521.5m (flattered by a one-off **IP-address sale**; CFO concedes "without the IP addresses, the EBITDA was coming in on a stable level only"). Dividend raised to €1.97, €100m buyback floated. waipu missed Vilanek's personal 2.0m target (landed 1.94m). First framing of waipu as an **EBITDA contributor**, not just a subscriber-growth story — a deliberate pivot to answer the bear ("waipu is a good product but cannot contribute EBITDA").
- **Q1'25 (May'25):** "sound start." Mobile net adds deliberately throttled ("we took money out of the market") to protect ARPU/margin — an offensive-sounding euphemism for a defensive volume decision. Vilanek's farewell call (16 yrs).
- **Q3'25 (Nov'25, new CEO Robin Harries):** Sharp tonal reset. "transformation into an **AI-first telco**," 50+ churn-reduction initiatives, web-shop conversion optimization, and the two structural moves that now dominate the thesis: the **mobilezone acquisition** (a ~1m-contracts/yr German sales machine; sparhandy/deinhandy brands) and the pending **CECONOMY stake exit** (~€150m in). The story expands from "harvest a mature cash cow" to "consolidate the German reseller channel + monetize the waipu ad-inventory + de-lever via asset sales."

**Drivers quietly dropped:** Gravis (shut); freenet-TV ever finding a "floor" (management has waited "7-8 years" and stopped pretending); the housing-association (Vonovia) distribution channel for waipu (explicitly abandoned — "it definitely doesn't work out"). **Drivers introduced:** CTV/addressable advertising on waipu (ProSiebenSat.1 deal live, ~30% of usage monetizable), Media Broadcast field-service pivot (EV-charger maintenance), mobilezone consolidation.

---

## 2. CLAIMS vs FINANCIALS (forensic)

| Mgmt claim | Quarter | Hard financials | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|
| "record year… record EBITDA >€520m" | FY'24 | NI 2024 €247m (vs €155m '23); EPS €2.08 | TRUE but **flattered** — CFO admits ex-IP-address sale EBITDA was merely *stable*. Story richer than the underlying. |
| Revenue "stable / growing" | all | Rev 2021 2.56B → 2024 2.48B → 2025 2.44B; **3-yr CAGR −1.5%** | DIVERGES. Group revenue is in slow secular decline. "Stable" is generous; mix-shift (high-margin service rev up, hardware/Gravis down) is the real story. |
| waipu "double-digit growth… 2.3m by end-'25, 3.5m by 2028" | Q1'24→Q3'25 | Net adds decelerated 139k (Q1'24) → 60k (Q1'25); 2.0m target *missed* (1.94m) | PARTLY DELIVERS but **serially shaved**. The growth is real but every milestone has been trimmed; "I personally believe in 2.3m" is conviction language, not a commitment. |
| "waipu will contribute significantly to EBITDA" | FY'24→Q3'25 | Group adj. EBITDA +1.6% 9M'25; Q3 +4% | CREDIBLE and improving — the one claim the numbers are starting to corroborate. |
| Mobile "small growth… stable ARPU" | all | Service rev grinding up low-single-digit; postpaid +182k FY'24, +190k 9M'25 | TRUE. Mobile is a low-growth, stable-margin annuity — exactly as described. |
| Capital discipline / FCF return | all | TTM FCF €344m; net margin 11.1%, FCF margin 14.9%; ROIC 10.6%, ROE 12.5% | TRUE. Genuinely cash-generative, asset-light. P/FCF 7.6x. The cash story is the most honest part of the deck. |
| €100m buyback | FY'24→Q3'25 | Executed only **~€60m** by Oct'25; "from today's view I would not expect additional buybacks this year" | **UNDER-DELIVERED.** The buyback is consistently floated at €100m and consistently capped at the €60m cash-overhang floor. A recurring credibility tell. |

**Net:** the financials are *solid but not exciting* — a flat-to-declining top line, stable-to-rising margins, strong FCF, modest deleveraging. Management's narrative is consistently ~10-15% richer than the trajectory justifies (record-year framing on a one-off; €100m buyback that's really €60m; waipu targets that keep slipping). Nothing fraudulent — a promotional-but-honest management that rounds up.

---

## 3. TONE & CONFIDENCE TRAJECTORY

**Trajectory: STABLE, with a late-window energy injection from the CEO change.**

- Q1'24 — *measured/defensive*: "a bit boring… happy that it's true." Honest about Gravis closure and mobile-market maturation.
- FY'24 — *peak confidence (slightly over-reaching)*: "record year," vanity metrics ("€961,500 FCF per shop opening day"). The IP-address caveat keeps it from tipping into spin.
- Q1'25 — *poised/defensive-dressed-as-offensive*: "we took out money of the market" — reframes a deliberate volume retreat as discipline. Farewell-CEO valedictory tone.
- Q3'25 — *re-energized/offensive*: Harries brings "AI-first telco," "final sprint," "50+ initiatives," and two concrete corporate actions (mobilezone, CECONOMY exit). Genuinely more specific and forward-leaning, but unproven.

Shift from offensive (waipu growth investment, FY'24) → defensive (ARPU protection, margin, "take money out of the market," Q1'25) → re-offensive (consolidation + efficiency + AI, Q3'25). Guidance specificity is *high on the near term* (annual EBITDA/FCF reliably hit) but *deliberately soft on the 2028 ambition* ("I tend to call it an ambition, not guidance"; "we will recalculate during 2026") — they protect optionality on the long-dated number.

---

## 4. GUIDANCE CREDIBILITY

**Reliability: HIGH on the annual P&L, MEDIUM on milestones and capital-return promises.**

- **Annual EBITDA/FCF guidance: consistently confirmed and hit.** Every call "fully confirms" the year's EBITDA guide and lands it. FY'25 guidance reaffirmed at Q3'25 and (per live verification) delivered — €2.07 dividend approved and paid May 2026, record postpaid + IPTV-EBITDA growth. This is the spine of the credibility case.
- **waipu subscriber milestones: serially missed/shaved** (2.0m → 1.94m; 2.3m "I personally believe"). Aspirational, not bankable.
- **Buyback: under-delivered** (€100m floated, ~€60m done).
- **Beat rate caveat:** the screener flags **beat 1/7 qtrs (14%)** — i.e., consensus EBITDA is set *at or above* where freenet lands; they meet but rarely *beat*. Note the latest-Q EPS came in **−17% YoY** and TTM diluted EPS (€2.21) is below the FY'25 annual EPS (€2.30), consistent with a tax-normalization drag (the tax-rate step-up from ~10.5% to ~20% post-2025 that the CFO flagged in Q1'24) and mobilezone integration/financing cost. **No sandbagging pattern** — if anything they guide tight and meet tight.

**Score: 4/5** — high follow-through on the cash and the dividend (the load-bearing claims for a deep-value name), docked one notch for the chronically-shaved buyback and waipu milestones.

---

## 5. ANALYST PRESSURE POINTS

Warburg's Simon Stippig (the persistent interlocutor across all four calls) and UBS repeatedly press on:

- **Buyback size & execution** — asked every call; management keeps the answer deliberately vague ("up to €100m… room for maneuver"). Later confirmed capped at ~€60m. *Pushed-on → became mild bad news.*
- **waipu net-add quality** — analysts probe whether growth is coming from low-ARPU entry products / klarmobil bundles (Q3'25, UBS Dhruva Shah: "how do you weigh ARPU vs volume"). Management deflects on absolute ARPU. *Live concern: growth-at-the-cost-of-mix.*
- **Nebenkostenprivileg / cable-migration tailwind** — analysts wanted to size the regulatory tailwind for waipu; Vilanek repeatedly downgraded it to "a silent impact… not a flood." *Pushed-on tailwind that never fully materialized.*
- **2028 ambition** — Stippig (Q3'25) pushed on whether mobilezone + marketing changes should *raise* the €600m ambition; CFO held it flat and punted to "recalculate during 2026." *Management protecting downside optionality.*
- **CECONOMY stake & financing** — analysts want to know if the ~€150m proceeds de-lever or fund the deal; CFO confirms "a slight up on the leverage."

**The thing analysts under-weight that management isn't volunteering:** the **tax-rate cliff** (10.5% → 20% from 2026, → ~30% from 2029). This is a structural EPS/FCF headwind that the cheerful EBITDA narrative papers over.

---

- **CECONOMY exit reframed from "strategic stake" to "source of cash."** In FY'24 Vilanek called CECONOMY "still a strategic investment… we'd be the last seller." By Q3'25 they are tendering the entire stake to JD.com at €4.60. The "strategic" framing was abandoned the moment a premium bid appeared — a useful read on how sticky management's "strategic" labels actually are (not very).
- **"Take money out of the market" (Q1'25)** is the single most revealing phrase — confirms freenet competes on *price discipline*, not product differentiation, in mobile. The mobile moat is thin and entirely about channel/scale, not switching costs.

---

## 8. CAPITAL-ALLOCATION VERDICT (for the director)

**Classification: STABLE, edging toward PIVOTING** — a mature, cash-generative MVNO consciously transitioning from "harvest + dividend" to "consolidate the reseller channel (mobilezone) + grow waipu into the EBITDA hole left by freenet-TV runoff + de-lever via the CECONOMY exit." The cash engine is genuine; the growth is modest and milestone-shaved; the long-dated 2028 ambition is held loosely.

**The single most important thing the director must weigh before sizing:** *this is a slow-compounding cash cow with a thin, channel-based moat and a melting legacy leg — not a special-situation with a live re-rating catalyst.* The headline corporate actions (mobilezone, CECONOMY, dividend, buyback) are **already largely spent or fixed**: mobilezone closed Dec'25, the dividend was paid May'26, the CECONOMY exit is a fixed-price tender (ARB, not re-rate), and the buyback is being shrunk. The investable question is purely *valuation vs. a no-growth, mildly-shrinking, tax-headwind'd FCF stream* — does ~7.6x P/FCF adequately discount the secular decline of the TV leg and the 2026→2029 tax-rate climb? At a 4% headline margin-of-safety to fair value, the answer is "barely."

**MOAT:** type = **scale-cost + thin channel/distribution intangible** (no network ownership, no switching-cost lock-in; the edge is reseller scale + retail/online footprint + the waipu product). The returns evidence — ROIC **10.6%** and ROE 12.5%, *stable not rising*, gross margin 25.9% "stable," net margin 11.1% — describes a **NARROW** moat that is **STABLE**, not widening. waipu adds a modestly differentiated product but no durable lock-in (customers churn freely; the whole mobile model is "match the most aggressive offer").

**SECULAR THREAT:** the relevant force is **linear-media-decline / cord-cutting** — but note the *nuance*: freenet sits on the **winning** side of cord-cutting via waipu.tv (IPTV is the substitute taking share from cable/linear) while bleeding on the **losing** side via freenet-TV (terrestrial DVB-T2) and Media Broadcast. Net, the secular force is **MANAGEABLE, not terminal** — the growth asset is the disruptor, the declining asset is a shrinking minority of EBITDA. The cheapness is therefore closer to a **mild fear-print on a no-growth profile** than a value trap, *provided* waipu's EBITDA ramp continues to outrun the TV runoff and the tax cliff. That proviso is the whole risk.

---

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 4 | TRAJECTORY: STABLE | MOAT: NARROW | MOAT_TREND: STABLE | SECULAR_THREAT: manageable

positioning_washout: Limited forced-selling dynamic. The shareholder base is yield/income-oriented (8.2% dividend, German retail + income funds), and the dividend is intact (EUR2.07, ~71% FCF payout) — so there is NO dividend-cut trigger to force mechanical liquidation in the base case. Conclusion: no positioning washout to harvest; the base is sticky on the yield, and the only flush scenario (div cut) is also a thesis-breaker, not an entry point.
moderator_conclusion: freenet is a well-run, genuinely cash-generative German MVNO + IPTV operator that is roughly FAIRLY valued, not a mispriced special situation — verdict C / conviction 2. The forensic record is solid-but-unspectacular (credibility 3): real ~EUR344m FCF, an ~8% covered dividend, waipu.tv now EBITDA-positive, and disciplined capital allocation (Gravis exit, price discipline). interrogator_dossier:
# Forensic Dossier — freenet AG (FNTN.DE)

*Communication Services · German MVNO + IPTV (waipu.tv) + Media Broadcast · deep-value signal*
*Transcripts: Q1-2024 (2024-05-17) → FY-2025 preliminary (2026-02-26). Financials: screener scan + FMP TTM as of 2026-03-31. Catalysts web-verified to 2026-06-21.*

---

## 1. NARRATIVE ARC

freenet's story across these eight quarters is the slow conversion of a **"boring, dividend-machine MVNO" into a self-styled "AI-first telco"** — a narrative escalation that is NOT matched by the financial trajectory.

- **Opening (Q1-2024, Vilanek):** The tone is deliberately, proudly dull. "I read a comment which said like everything on plan and a bit boring. I think that's true, but I'm happy that it's true." The thesis is three legs: (1) a mature, cash-generative **mobile MVNO** reselling DT/Vodafone/Telefónica airtime; (2) **waipu.tv**, the IPTV growth engine, then crossing 1.5M subs with ~140k record net adds; (3) **Media Broadcast** (terrestrial DVB-T/DAB), explicitly "shrinking as planned," ~EUR100m EBITDA, being repurposed into field-service B2B. Capital allocation is the headline draw: 80% of FCF paid as dividend, plus a buyback.
- **The Gravis exit (Q1-2024):** Management cleanly euthanized the loss-making Apple-reseller Gravis ("two companies that suffer from the same disease… would not create enough synergies"). A green flag for discipline — they kill what doesn't earn.
- **FY-2024 (2025-03-05):** Peak confidence. Vilanek's valedictory ("record year 2024"), EBITDA EUR521.5m (flattered by a one-off IP-address sale), dividend raised to EUR1.97, EUR100m buyback teased. waipu missed his "personal ambition" of 2M (landed 1.94M) — the first crack in the IPTV story.
- **CEO transition (mid-2025):** Vilanek (16 years, 63 calls) hands to **Robin Harries**. The narrative immediately re-skins: "transformation into an **AI-first telco**," "50+ churn-reduction initiatives," "AI voice bot," "customer value management." This is a tonal pivot from *operational steadiness* to *transformation/initiative* language — classic new-CEO repositioning.
- **mobilezone acquisition (Q3-2025 onward):** The biggest strategic shift — buying a direct competitor ("a sales machine," 1M+ contracts/yr) for ~EUR230m, debt-financed via bridge loan. The story expands from organic-MVNO to **roll-up consolidator**.
- **FY-2025 preliminary (2026-02-26):** The narrative quietly turns **defensive**. Harries leads not with growth but with a confession: *"we are not happy about agreement with the network provider… up to EUR 50 million negative EBITDA impact for the year '26 to '28."* The pricing-discipline slide ("quality over quantity," raising frontbook prices) replaces the land-grab tone. Guidance for 2026 is **"moderate decrease."**

**Net arc:** from boring-but-reliable cash compounder → growth-via-waipu → AI-transformation + M&A roll-up, even as the core financials flatlined and a structural MNO-contract headwind emerged. The label inflated faster than the earnings.

---

## 2. CLAIMS vs FINANCIALS (forensic)

| Management claim | Quarter | Hard financial evidence | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|
| "Record year 2024" | FY-24 | EBITDA EUR521.5m **but adjusted-EBITDA flat ex IP-address sale** (Arnold's own caveat); revenue 2.48B vs 2.56B in 2021 | **Poorer than billed** — the "record" leaned on a one-off; revenue is *lower* than 4 years prior |
| Strong customer/revenue growth | FY-25/Q3-25 | Revenue **-1.5% YoY, 3-yr CAGR -1.5%**; FY trajectory 2.56→2.56→2.63→2.48→**2.44B** | **Diverges** — subs grow, revenue shrinks; ARPU/mix offsetting volume |
| waipu.tv "fantastic," profitable growth | Q3-25/FY-25 | ~EUR36m adj-EBITDA in 2025 (real, confirmed); but 2.0M target missed (1.94M), then 2028 sub target effectively trimmed (3.5M→3.0M) | **Mixed** — EBITDA proof real; sub-ambition quietly walked back |
| Margins healthy/expanding | FY-25 | Gross 25.9% (stable), Net 11.1%, FCF 14.9%; ROE 12.5%, **ROIC 10.6%** | **Matches** — genuinely strong cash conversion for an asset-light reseller; but margins ~0.53σ *below* historic peak |
| EPS momentum | FY-25 | EPS 1.62→0.67→1.30→2.08→2.30; +10.6% YoY, but **latest-Q EPS YoY -17%** and **TTM diluted EPS 2.21** (below the 2.30 FY print) | **Deteriorating at the margin** — the most recent quarter inflected down |
| "Up to EUR100m buyback" | FY-24→Q3-25 | Only ~**EUR60m** executed; Arnold: *"from today's point of view, I would not expect any additional share buybacks"* | **Under-delivered** — the EUR100m was aspirational; real return = the EUR60m FCF overhang |
| Net cash / low leverage | various | **NET DEBT EUR596m** recomputed from 2026-03-31 balance sheet (EUR694m debt − EUR98m cash); mobilezone bridge loan adds leverage | **Liability, not cushion** — must be SUBTRACTED in any SoP |
| "Earnings beat" reliability | — | Screener: **beat 1/7 qtrs (14%)** | **Weak** — management consistently lands near/below consensus on EBITDA |

The single most important divergence: **the narrative grew (AI-telco, roll-up, transformation) while revenue went backwards and the most recent quarter's EPS fell 17% YoY.** The cash machine is real; the growth story is largely a re-labeling.

---

## 3. TONE & CONFIDENCE TRAJECTORY

**Trajectory: DECLINING (offensive → defensive), masked by a louder transformation vocabulary.**

- **Q1-2024 (Rising/Stable, offensive):** *"happy that it's [boring]… no doubt about the guidance."* Confident, growth-investing in waipu.
- **FY-2024 (Peak):** *"record year… remarkable numbers."* Highest confidence of the set.
- **Q1/H1-2025 (Stable, hedged):** *"we have… consequently taken out money of the market… we did on purpose take down the numbers a little bit."* Defensive framing of a *deliberate* slowdown — plausible, but a tone-shift from land-grab to discipline.
- **Q3-2025 (new CEO, superficially Rising):** Harries floods the call with initiative-language — *"AI-first telco… 50+ initiatives… just getting started."* High energy, but notably **vague on quantification** ("conversion rates… not great yet," "huge potential").
- **FY-2025 prelim (Declining, defensive):** The lead message is a problem, not a win: *"we are not happy about agreement with the network provider."* Guidance is *"moderate decrease."* The pricing-discipline pivot (*"quality is more important than quantity"*) is the language of a company protecting margin in a saturating market, not expanding.

The vocabulary got more ambitious (AI, transformation) exactly as the substance got more defensive (MNO headwind, price discipline, sub-target trims). **That divergence is itself the tell.**

---

## 4. GUIDANCE CREDIBILITY

**Reliability: MEDIUM.**

- **Delivered:** EBITDA guidance ("around EUR520m" range) was hit across 2024-2025; FCF guidance broadly met; waipu *EBITDA* contribution (a stated 2025 goal) materialized (~EUR36m); 2025 guidance "confirmed" every quarter and met.
- **Missed/walked back:** waipu **2.0M** sub target (Vilanek's "personal ambition") missed; **2.3M** 2025 target became a guided 2.2M; **3.5M** 2028 ambition slid to ~3.0M. The **EUR100m buyback** became EUR60m.
- **Beat rate:** screener says **beat 1/7 qtrs (14%)** — management lands *on* plan, not above it. No sandbagging pattern; if anything they set ambitions (sub targets) they then trim.
- **Big credibility test now live:** the **MNO contract renegotiation**. Management is "very optimistic" but has **no legal leverage** to compel better tariffs (see §5). Putting the EUR50m headwind *into* guidance is honest; promising a renegotiated fix is not yet credible.

Net: they hit the **financial** guidance they control (EBITDA/FCF/dividend) but repeatedly trim the **operational ambitions** (subs, buyback) they aspire to. Reliable on the floor, unreliable on the ceiling.

---

## 5. ANALYST PRESSURE POINTS

- **The MNO/Telefónica contract (the dominant 2026 pressure point).** Joshua Mills (BNP Paribas, FY-25) drilled precisely into the weak spot: *"if that operator isn't giving you those tariffs… what levers and what legal backing do you have to demand access?"* and *"there's no guarantee on paying a certain amount to freenet."* **Management deflected** — Arnold: *"About the legal actions and possibilities, I do not want to talk about… we see possibilities there."* This is a **non-answer on the single biggest EBITDA risk.** The "we're optimistic, in fruitful discussions" framing is exactly what a party with no contractual leverage says.
- **waipu sub-target trims.** UBS and Citi repeatedly pressed on whether the better waipu *EBITDA* assumption is funded by *fewer* subs (ARPU-up vs volume-down). Management waved it off ("still 3 million") but the trajectory of trims validates the worry.
- **Buyback size.** Simon Stippig (Warburg) pushed three separate calls on EUR60m vs EUR100m; management consistently retreated to "based on cash development."
- **Nebenkostenprivileg / cord-cutting tailwind** that never fully arrived: Vilanek admitted across multiple calls *"we still have not seen a straight impact or measurable impact"* — a promised waipu accelerant that stayed "silent."
- **CECONOMY stake** — analysts probed it as a hidden asset; management called it "strategic," "last seller" — then sold the entire stake into JD.com's bid (see §6, §8).

---

## 6. RED FLAGS / GREEN FLAGS

🔴 **Revenue in structural reverse** — 2.56B (2021) → 2.44B (2025), -1.5% 3-yr CAGR; subs grow but top line shrinks (ARPU/mix/Gravis-exit drag).
🔴 **MNO contract = up to EUR50m EBITDA hole (2026-28) with no legal leverage to fix it** — management deflected the legal-backing question.
🔴 **Latest-Q EPS -17% YoY; TTM EPS 2.21 < FY 2.30** — the most recent inflection is *down*.
🔴 **"Record year" flattered by a one-off** IP-address sale (adj-EBITDA was flat) — a quality-of-earnings caution.
🔴 **Leverage rising into a deal** — mobilezone bridge-financed; net debt EUR596m; buyback cut to EUR60m partly to preserve balance-sheet room.
🔴 **Beat rate 14% (1/7)** — never positively surprises.
🔴 **New-CEO narrative inflation** — "AI-first telco" vocabulary outrunning a flat-revenue reality.
🔴 **Media Broadcast in secular terminal decline** (terrestrial TV) — managed, but a melting sub-segment.

🟢 **Genuine, durable FCF** — P/FCF 7.5x, FCF margin ~15%, TTM FCF EUR344m; ROIC 10.6% on an asset-light base.
🟢 **Capital-allocation discipline** — killed Gravis cleanly; ~80% FCF dividend; opportunistic buyback.
🟢 **waipu.tv reached EBITDA profitability** (~EUR36m 2025) — the growth leg now *pays*, not just grows.
🟢 **Pricing discipline working** — frontbook prices raised through Cyber Week 2025 without a churn spike; "quality over quantity."
🟢 **Multi-method MoS positive** — DCF +37%, EPV +30%, OwnerEarnings +45%, Graham +60% all agree it's cheap (only IV15 dissents).
🟢 **CECONOMY stake monetization** — entire stake tendered into JD.com's EUR4.60/share offer, ~EUR150m cash inflow pending close (H1-2026).

---

## 7. HIDDEN SIGNALS

- **Terminology shift = thesis shift.** Vilanek's "boring," "hygiene factor," "remarkable cash" → Harries's "AI-first," "performance marketing," "return on ad spend," "customer value management." The company is repositioning its *equity story* (for a higher multiple) more than its *economics*.
- **CECONOMY about-face.** In FY-2024 Vilanek: *"we would be the last one to give away our shares… I would call it still a strategic investment."* By H2-2025 freenet had **committed its entire stake to JD.com's tender**. The "strategic forever-hold" became a seller the moment a real bid arrived — pragmatic, but it exposes the "strategic" label as negotiable.
- **mobilezone = defensive consolidation dressed as growth.** Buying your strongest competitor in a *shrinking-revenue* market is as much margin-defense/scale-rationalization as expansion; the "synergies" language (50 unspecified examples, "low-hanging fruit") is thin.
- **The buyback retreat** (EUR100m → EUR60m → "no further") quietly signals the bridge loan and dividend leave little surplus — capital is tighter than the dividend headline implies.
- **"Moderate decrease" guidance for 2026** is the first explicitly down-guide of the set — buried late in the FY-25 prelim call, not led with.

## 8. CAPITAL-ALLOCATION VERDICT (for the director)

**Classification: STABLE, drifting toward PIVOTING.** This is a mature, cash-generative, well-managed MVNO whose *core economics are stable-to-gently-eroding* and whose *narrative is pivoting* (new CEO, AI-telco re-skin, M&A roll-up). It is neither strengthening (revenue is flat-down, EBITDA faces a EUR50m contract hole) nor deteriorating outright (FCF and dividend are intact, waipu now earns).

**The single most important thing to weigh before sizing:** the **MNO/Telefónica contract** is a binding, dated (2026-2028) EUR-up-to-50m EBITDA headwind on which management has **already conceded it has no contractual leverage** — the "renegotiation" is a hope, not a catalyst. The deep-value case rests on (a) durable FCF that survives this hole, and (b) the **CECONOMY/JD.com cash realization (~EUR150m, H1-2026)** — which is a near-locked ARB-style inflow (terms fixed at EUR4.60), NOT an asymmetric re-rate. Do not size this as a special-situation alpha bet; size it as a *cheap, low-growth, decent-FCF reseller with a contract overhang and one clean cash crystallization pending.*

**ECONOMIC MOAT.** Type: **regulatory-license / scale-distribution (NARROW).** freenet owns no spectrum or network — its "moat" is its position as Germany's largest independent MVNO/distributor (scale in retail + customer base, the legal right to resell on all three MNOs) plus waipu.tv's brand/product lead in IPTV. The MNO contract dispute exposes the core fragility: **its economics are at the mercy of the network owners' tariff terms.** ROIC 10.6% and ROE 12.5% are *solid and roughly stable* (not rising) — this is a NARROW moat, STABLE-to-mild-eroding, not a widening one. Gross margin 25.9% is "stable" but ~0.53σ below historic peak; the MNO headwind will press it.

**SECULAR THREAT.** Two structural forces touch it but neither is clearly *terminal*: (1) **linear-media decline / cord-cutting** hits the legacy *freenet TV* (terrestrial DVB-T) and Media Broadcast sub-segments — but freenet is on the *winning* side of cord-cutting via waipu.tv (IPTV gaining as linear fades), so the net effect is **manageable**, even mildly favorable, for the TV segment overall. (2) The mobile MVNO faces a **saturated, deflationary German telco market** (flat revenue, price competition) and **structural dependence on MNO goodwill** — a *material* margin risk but not a terminal-value wipeout. There is no AI-displacement or disintermediation that voids the franchise. Cheapness here is closer to a **mature-low-growth fear-print with a known contract overhang** than a melting-base value trap — but it is *not* a hidden compounder either.
